title: Runtime Sanitizers, Dynamic Analysis, and Language Profiles · Signal Triage
summary: Add runtime instrumentation to native and managed-language test profiles. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
Select thread, memory, leak, race, or platform sanitizers according to risk and compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, apply Asan without its stated preconditions, omit the a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Asan for a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix.
The record names the assumption, applies Asan, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Asan record says a sanitizer and dynamic-analysis matrix looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a gates-sanitizer-ci-18rjrc: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sanitizer Ci into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-sanitizer-ci-18rjrc:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
